Guideline: Define tables with pgTable, typed columns, and indexes
Description: Use pgTable to define your database schema with strongly-typed columns, constraints, and indexes. Keep schema files modular — one file per domain entity.
Do: Use pgTable with explicit column types and constraints; add indexes for frequently queried columns
Dont: Dont use raw SQL strings for schema; dont skip NOT NULL constraints on required fields

Guideline: Initialize drizzle with a pg Pool connection
Description: Create a single drizzle client instance using a PostgreSQL connection pool. Export it for reuse across your application. Never create multiple drizzle instances.
Do: Create one drizzle instance with a connection pool; export it as a singleton
Dont: Dont create a new drizzle client per request; dont use a single connection instead of a pool

Guideline: Use db.select().from() for type-safe queries
Description: Build SELECT queries using the fluent query builder. Use .select() with specific columns to avoid fetching unnecessary data. Chain .where() for filtering.
Do: Select only needed columns; use the query builder for type safety
Dont: Dont select all columns when you only need a few; dont build queries with string concatenation

Guideline: Use db.insert(table).values() with returning()
Description: Insert rows using the type-safe .insert().values() method. Always use .returning() when you need the inserted row back. Use the inferred insert type for input validation.
Do: Use .returning() to get inserted data; validate input types with InferInsertModel
Dont: Dont manually construct INSERT SQL; dont skip returning() when you need the new row

Guideline: Use db.update(table).set().where() with returning()
Description: Update rows using the fluent .update().set().where() chain. Always include a .where() clause to avoid updating all rows. Use .returning() to confirm changes.
Do: Always include a where clause; use returning() to confirm the update
Dont: Dont update without a where clause; dont assume the update succeeded without checking

Guideline: Use db.delete(table).where() with a mandatory filter
Description: Delete rows using .delete().where(). Never call delete without a where clause. Use .returning() if you need to confirm what was deleted.
Do: Always use a where clause; use returning() to verify deletion
Dont: Dont delete without a where clause; dont ignore the deletion result

Guideline: Use eq, ne, gt, lt, like, and, or for type-safe filters
Description: Combine Drizzle filter operators for complex WHERE conditions. Use and() and or() to compose multiple conditions. All operators are fully type-safe.
Do: Use typed operators like eq, gt, like; compose with and/or
Dont: Dont use raw SQL strings for conditions; dont nest conditions without and/or helpers

Guideline: Use innerJoin, leftJoin, rightJoin for multi-table queries
Description: Join tables using the fluent join methods. Specify the join condition with the on parameter. Alias columns to avoid name collisions.
Do: Use typed join methods with explicit on conditions; alias overlapping columns
Dont: Dont use raw SQL for joins; dont forget to handle null results from left joins

Guideline: Define relations() for type-safe relational queries
Description: Use the relations() helper to declare one-to-many and many-to-many relationships. This enables the relational query API with db.query for nested data fetching.
Do: Define relations in schema; use db.query for nested fetches
Dont: Dont skip relation definitions; dont use manual joins when relational queries suffice

Guideline: Use db.$with for Common Table Expressions (CTEs)
Description: Use $with to create named subqueries (CTEs) for complex queries. This improves readability and allows reuse of intermediate results.
Do: Use $with for CTEs; keep subqueries readable and named
Dont: Dont inline complex subqueries; dont nest more than 2-3 levels deep

Guideline: Use db.transaction() for atomic multi-step operations
Description: Wrap related database operations in a transaction to ensure atomicity. Use the provided tx object for all queries within the transaction. Throw an error to trigger a rollback.
Do: Use db.transaction with the tx parameter; throw to rollback
Dont: Dont mix db and tx inside a transaction; dont swallow errors silently

Guideline: Use drizzle-kit generate and migrate for schema changes
Description: Generate SQL migration files from schema changes using drizzle-kit generate. Apply them with drizzle-kit migrate or programmatically with the migrate() function.
Do: Use drizzle-kit generate for migrations; review generated SQL before applying
Dont: Dont push directly to production; dont skip reviewing migration SQL

Guideline: Use drizzle-kit push only in development for rapid iteration
Description: drizzle-kit push applies schema changes directly to the database without generating migration files. Use it for fast development iteration only — never in production.
Do: Use push for local development; use generate/migrate for staging and production
Dont: Dont use push in production; dont rely on push for team collaboration

Guideline: Use .prepare() for frequently executed queries
Description: Prepare statements that run often to skip the query planning phase on subsequent executions. Use placeholder() for dynamic parameters.
Do: Prepare hot-path queries; use placeholder for dynamic values
Dont: Dont prepare queries that run once; dont hardcode values in prepared statements

Guideline: Use the sql`` template literal for complex expressions
Description: Use the sql tagged template for raw SQL expressions within Drizzle queries. This is useful for database functions, complex calculations, and features not yet covered by the query builder.
Do: Use sql`` with typed generics; embed in select/where/orderBy
Dont: Dont use string interpolation for user input in sql``; dont bypass the query builder unnecessarily

Guideline: Use count, sum, avg with groupBy for aggregate queries
Description: Use Drizzle aggregate functions with .groupBy() for summarized data. Combine with .having() to filter aggregated results.
Do: Use typed aggregate functions; combine groupBy with having
Dont: Dont aggregate in application code; dont forget groupBy with aggregate functions

Guideline: Use limit/offset or cursor-based pagination
Description: Implement offset-based pagination for simple cases and cursor-based for large datasets. Cursor-based pagination avoids the performance issues of high offsets.
Do: Use cursor-based pagination for large datasets; always limit results
Dont: Dont use high offsets on large tables; dont fetch all rows and paginate in JS

Guideline: Use orderBy with asc/desc for sorted results
Description: Apply ordering using the asc() and desc() helpers from drizzle-orm. Support multiple sort columns for deterministic ordering.
Do: Use asc/desc helpers; include a unique column as tiebreaker
Dont: Dont rely on database default ordering; dont use raw SQL for simple ordering

Guideline: Use drizzle-zod for automatic schema validation
Description: Generate Zod schemas from your Drizzle table definitions using drizzle-zod. Use createInsertSchema and createSelectSchema for input validation and API response typing.
Do: Use createInsertSchema for input validation; refine schemas with Zod methods
Dont: Dont write Zod schemas manually when drizzle-zod can generate them; dont skip validation

Guideline: Use InferSelectModel and InferInsertModel for derived types
Description: Derive TypeScript types directly from your Drizzle schema using InferSelectModel and InferInsertModel. Never manually define types that mirror your table schema.
Do: Use InferSelectModel/InferInsertModel; derive types from the schema
Dont: Dont manually define types that duplicate the schema; dont use any for database results

Guideline: Configure pg Pool with proper limits and timeouts
Description: Set appropriate connection pool size based on your environment. Configure idle timeout and connection timeout to handle failures gracefully. Always close the pool on shutdown.
Do: Set max connections based on environment; handle pool shutdown gracefully
Dont: Dont use unlimited connections; dont skip error handling on the pool

Guideline: Use db.insert().values([...]) for efficient bulk inserts
Description: Insert multiple rows in a single query by passing an array to .values(). For very large datasets chunk the inserts to avoid exceeding query parameter limits.
Do: Batch inserts with array values; chunk large datasets
Dont: Dont insert rows one at a time in a loop; dont pass unbounded arrays

Guideline: Use onConflictDoUpdate for insert-or-update patterns
Description: Use .onConflictDoUpdate() to handle unique constraint conflicts gracefully. Specify the conflict target and the columns to update on conflict.
Do: Use onConflictDoUpdate with explicit target; update only necessary columns
Dont: Dont catch unique constraint errors manually; dont use onConflictDoNothing when you need updates

Guideline: Use in-memory SQLite or test containers for database tests
Description: Test your Drizzle queries against a real database. Use SQLite in-memory for fast unit tests and PostgreSQL test containers for integration tests. Apply migrations before each test suite.
Do: Use real database for tests; isolate test data with transactions
Dont: Dont mock the database layer; dont run tests against production data

Guideline: Choose query builder vs raw SQL based on query complexity
Description: Use the Drizzle query builder for standard CRUD operations and switch to raw sql`` for performance-critical or highly complex queries. Monitor slow queries and use EXPLAIN ANALYZE.
Do: Profile queries with EXPLAIN ANALYZE; use query builder first then optimize
Dont: Dont prematurely optimize with raw SQL; dont ignore slow query logs
